skill.name: postdoc-fellowship-matcher
skill.description: Filter and match postdoctoral fellowship opportunities based on applicant nationality, years since PhD, and research field from a curated database.
skill.summary: Match postdoctoral fellowship opportunities to applicants by filtering a curated database based on nationality, career stage, and research field.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 54 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. External_commands findings reference markdown code examples in documentation files, not executable shell commands. Network findings point to legitimate reference URLs for fellowship programs (NIH, NSF, HFSP, EMBO, MSCA, Schmidt) in documentation files. Weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false pattern matches from audit JSON content. The Python script (scripts/main.py) performs local fellowship filtering with no network calls, credential handling, or dynamic code execution. No prompt injection or data exfiltration patterns were detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown code examples flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Backtick-fenced code blocks in SKILL.md and POLISH_CHANGELOG.md show usage examples (e.g., python scripts/main.py --help). These are documentation formatting, not shell execution. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs flagged as network risk
low_findings.description: URLs in references/fellowships.md and SKILL.md point to legitimate fellowship program websites (NIH, NSF, HFSP, EMBO) and the source GitHub repository. These are reference data, not executable network code. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Audit JSON and argparse misidentified as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged text in postdoc-fellowship-matcher_audit_result_v2.json and scripts/main.py:81 as weak cryptographic algorithm. The audit JSON contains evaluation scores and pass/fail results. scripts/main.py:81 is an argparse.ArgumentParser() call. No cryptographic code exists in this skill. FALSE POSITIVE.

content.user_title: Match Postdoctoral Fellowships to Your Profile
content.value_statement: Postdoc applicants face a fragmented landscape of fellowship programs, each with different nationality, career stage, and field requirements. This skill filters a curated fellowship database against your personal criteria and returns only eligible opportunities with deadlines and requirements.
actual_capabilities: Filter fellowships by applicant nationality, years since PhD, and research field
Display ranked eligible opportunities with deadlines and key requirements
Normalize common research field aliases (e.g., neuro to neuroscience)
Provide match rationale for each listed fellowship
Include eligibility caveats and verification steps in every result
Refuse out-of-scope requests such as application writing or career counseling
limitations: Fellowship data comes from a built-in database, not a live source with real-time updates
Cannot draft fellowship applications, personal statements, or provide career counseling
Nationality codes require standard two-letter format (US, CN, DE) with no alias normalization
Eligibility output is a filter result, not an official or legal determination

use_cases.title: International Postdoc Applicant
use_cases.description: A researcher moving to a new country needs to find fellowships open to their nationality and career stage across multiple funding programs.

use_cases.title: Career Development Advisor
use_cases.description: An advisor needs to quickly narrow down fellowship options for multiple advisees with different profiles in a research office setting.
use_cases.target_user: Postdoc office staff and research mentors

use_cases.title: Funding Landscape Survey
use_cases.description: A late-stage PhD candidate exploring postdoc options wants to understand which fellowships they will be eligible for after graduation.
use_cases.target_user: Senior PhD candidates planning postdoctoral training

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fellowship Search
prompt_templates.scenario: New user looking for immediate fellowship matches
prompt_templates.prompt: I am a postdoc applicant. My nationality is US, I am 2 years post-PhD, and my research field is immunology. What fellowships am I eligible for?

prompt_templates.title: Personalized Report with Name
prompt_templates.scenario: User who wants a named report header for records
prompt_templates.prompt: My name is Dr. Jane Smith, my nationality is CN, I am 3 years post-PhD in structural biology. Please find matching fellowships and include a header with my name.

prompt_templates.title: International Applicant Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: What fellowships can I apply for with German nationality, 1 year post-PhD, in neuroscience? I need programs that accept international applicants.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Multi-Constraint Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needing structured output with assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Match fellowships for a UK national, 4 years post-PhD in cell biology. Include your assumptions, the filter criteria applied, and any eligibility caveats I should verify before applying.

output_examples.input: My nationality is US, I am 1 year post-PhD, and my research field is immunology. What fellowships am I eligible for?
output: NIH F32 - Eligible: US citizens and permanent residents. Deadline: April/October/December. Duration: Up to 3 years.
NSF Postdoc - Eligible: US citizens and permanent residents. Deadline: October. Duration: 2-3 years.
HFSP Fellowship - Eligible: Must change country for postdoc. Deadline: March/August. Duration: 3 years.
Note: Verify current deadlines on official program websites before applying.

output_examples.input: I am a Chinese national, 3 years post-PhD in structural biology. Find matching fellowships.
output: HFSP Fellowship - Eligible: Must change country for postdoc. Deadline: March/August. Duration: 3 years.
EMBO Fellowship - Eligible: Must move to EMBC member state. Deadline: February/August. Duration: Up to 2 years.
Note: US-citizen-only fellowships (NIH F32, NSF PRFB) were filtered out based on your nationality. Verify mobility requirements on official program sites.

best_practices: Verify current deadlines and requirements on official program websites before applying, as the database may not reflect the latest updates
Provide your full nationality, years since PhD, and research field for the most accurate filtering results
Use common field name aliases such as neuro for neuroscience or immuno for immunology, and the skill will normalize them automatically
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to write fellowship applications or personal statements, which is outside its documented scope
Treating the skills output as an official eligibility determination without verifying on program websites
Using unrecognized nationality formats like American instead of US or Chinese instead of CN instead of standard two-letter codes

faq.question: What types of fellowships does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers NIH F32, NSF Postdoctoral Fellowships, HFSP Fellowship, EMBO Fellowship, Marie Curie Actions, and Schmidt Science Fellows. See references/fellowships.md for full details.

faq.question: Does this skill access live fellowship databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses a built-in static database. Always verify deadlines and requirements on official program websites before applying.

faq.question: Can this skill help me write a fellowship application?
faq.answer: No. The skill only filters fellowship opportunities based on your profile. For application writing support, use an academic writing skill or consult your institution postdoc office.

faq.question: How do I enter my nationality?
faq.answer: Use standard two-letter country codes such as US, CN, DE, or UK. Avoid long-form names like American or Chinese for consistent matching.

faq.question: What happens if my research field is not in the alias table?
faq.answer: The skill will return the closest matching fields and ask you to confirm. It normalizes common aliases such as neuro to neuroscience automatically.

faq.question: Is the eligibility information guaranteed to be accurate?
faq.answer: The eligibility information is based on the built-in database and is a filter result, not an official determination. Always check the official program guidelines before applying.
